title: N Name table
text: N Name table Construct the name of the overflow table. Modes None. Conditions None. Description This request parses the table name to determine the position of the ‘O’ indicating an overflow table, and changes the table name. If an occurrence of an entity containing variable-length fields exceeds 8192 bytes, Uniface creates overflow segments that are stored in overflow tables. The overflow table or file has the same name as the main entity, preceded by an ‘O’. The syntax of table names can be too complex to let Uniface place this ‘O’. An example of this is when database, catalogs, database schema names, and separators for these items are included in the entity name. In this case, the Name table request can be used to let the connector place the ‘O’. The request is provided with a dummy control block. This control block does not describe a table and it does not have an associated connector workspace. Refer to: Uniface Database I/O Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
